explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the autopilot will eventually change to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3), either immediately or within a certain duration (N_DURATION). This captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions to NOMINAL under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that the autopilot's state will eventually become NOMINAL if the system is supported and sensor data is good. The property uses the G operator to ensure this condition always holds, and the F operator to ensure that at some point in the future the state will be NOMINAL. The boolean expressions are derived from the provided atomic propositions.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the state changes from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. This is captured by the template 'G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3)))'. The property is satisfied if the system is in nominal or transition state if supported and sensor data is good.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot state will be NOMINAL. This is captured by using the formula G( (supported & good) -> F state_is_NOMINAL).

The LTL property states that globally, if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state. This directly reflects the requirement that the autopilot transitions to NOMINAL under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot's state will be NOMINAL. The property uses the 'G' (Globally) and 'F' (Eventually) operators to express this requirement. The boolean expressions are defined in terms of atomic propositions representing system health, sensor data, and autopilot state.

The autopilot state will eventually become NOMINAL if it is currently in the TRANSITION state, the system is supported, and the sensor data is good. This is captured by the chosen LTL template. The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the autopilot is in TRANSITION, supported and good, then eventually it will be in the NOMINAL state. The boolean expressions are built upon the provided atomic propositions.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then it should transition to NOMINAL state. This is captured using the 'G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3)))' template where bool_exp2 is (supported & good), and bool_exp3 is state_is_NOMINAL

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL.  The property uses a global implication, and the consequent makes use of a future operator to represent the eventuality.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the state is TRANSITION and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the state becomes NOMINAL.  The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this holds true at all times.  The '->' (Implication) sets up the condition, and the 'F' (Finally) with 'state_is_NOMINAL' specifies that the system transitions to NOMINAL given the conditions.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the nominal state. Specifically, the property ensures that whenever 'supported' and 'good' are true, 'state_is_NOMINAL' will eventually become true.  The use of 'X' (next) is important to allow the model to move into the target state.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the state is TRANSITION, the system is supported, and sensor data is good, then eventually, within a certain time frame (N_DURATION), the state will transition to NOMINAL. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should switch to NOMINAL when conditions are met.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) the autopilot state will be NOMINAL (bool_exp3) or if the state is already NOMINAL then the autopilot will be in NOMINAL state. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition to NOMINAL state under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually transition to NOMINAL. The property uses the LTL template to define this behavior. bool_exp1 represents the condition for 'supported' and 'good' sensor data, bool_exp2 is always true in this case, and bool_exp3 represents the 'state_is_NOMINAL' condition. The constant N_DURATION is set to 1 to define the time window.

This LTL property states that globally, if the state is TRANSITION and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually (within a fixed time, N_DURATION) the state will become NOMINAL. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition to the NOMINAL state when the conditions are met.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the state will transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL (X state_is_NOMINAL), which corresponds to the autopilot changing states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. The use of the next operator(X) expresses the desired behavior will be achieved in the next step. Also, the LTL property ensures that this transition is maintained over a time period. This is captured by using G.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL.  The property uses the G (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true at all times.  The implication is that if the condition is true, then the action must eventually be performed, making the property describe the system behavior.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot state will change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. This is captured by using the 'G' (Globally) operator. The implication operator '->' is used with the condition and the action. The bool_exp1 and bool_exp2 are placeholders in the LTL template which represent conditions to be satisfied. bool_exp3 represents the desired state to be reached. We choose template 10 and set N_DURATION to 1.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then it is always the case that the autopilot changes states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. This is captured by the boolean expression `state_is_TRANSITION` indicating the current state is TRANSITION, `state_is_NOMINAL` indicating the current state is NOMINAL, `supported` indicating the system is supported, and `good` indicating that sensor data is good.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the state will eventually become NOMINAL (F[0, N_DURATION] bool_exp3).  This corresponds to the requirement that the autopilot changes state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. The duration, N_DURATION, provides a bounded time frame for the state change to occur.  bool_exp2 is the antecedent (condition), and the consequent is the change of state. The condition should always imply the change of state.

The autopilot eventually transitions to the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL) when the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good). This is expressed using the G (Globally) operator, which combined with the boolean expression ensures that the condition (supported & good) leads to eventually state_is_NOMINAL.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), and the state is in TRANSITION (bool_exp1), then it will eventually transition to NOMINAL (bool_exp3) or stay in NOMINAL for a certain duration. The property uses a bounded future operator to ensure a timely transition. N_DURATION defines the duration. bool_exp4 is not used.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually, the autopilot state transitions to NOMINAL, or NOMINAL remains active for at least N_DURATION time steps. This directly reflects the requirement that the autopilot changes state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL under the given conditions.

The LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION), and the system is supported (supported) and the sensor data is good (good), then eventually (F) the autopilot will transition to NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL).  This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition to NOMINAL state when the mentioned conditions are met. N_DURATION is set to 1 for a minimal time frame.

The LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION) and the system is supported and sensor data is good (supported & good), then the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state (X state_is_NOMINAL).  The property uses the Next operator (X) to capture the requirement that the state change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change its state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. This is captured by the chosen template. The boolean expressions are defined using the atomic propositions as per the prompt's instructions.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) the autopilot state will become NOMINAL (bool_exp3). This property ensures that the state transition to NOMINAL happens when both conditions, system support and good sensor data, are met.

The LTL property ensures that globally, whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state. This is captured by using G (Globally) and F (Eventually) along with the implication operator, which defines the condition for the transition.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the state_is_NOMINAL (bool_exp3) will be true after a certain duration and/or globally. The duration is specified by N_DURATION. This captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions to the NOMINAL state under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the autopilot will eventually change its state to NOMINAL (F state_is_NOMINAL) within a certain time frame (N_DURATION). It ensures the autopilot transitions to NOMINAL when the conditions are met.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change to the NOMINAL state. The property uses a template that checks for a condition (supported & good) to lead to another condition (state_is_NOMINAL).

This LTL property states that if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state. The G operator means always, the -> means implication, and the F operator means eventually.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot state will be NOMINAL.  The boolean expressions define the conditions for support, good sensor data, and the autopilot's states.

This LTL property states that globally, whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition to NOMINAL state. The chosen template is `G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3)))`.  `bool_exp2` represents the condition `supported & good`. `bool_exp3` represents the state_is_NOMINAL.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then eventually the autopilot's state will become NOMINAL, or will be NOMINAL for N_DURATION time units. The LTL then ensures that the autopilot transitions to NOMINAL when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.

This LTL property states that globally, if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot will transition to NOMINAL state. The boolean expressions reflect that the state should transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL given the conditions.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then the autopilot state will eventually transition to NOMINAL. Specifically, the autopilot transitions from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when supported and good. The use of G (globally) ensures that this condition must always hold, whenever supported and good are true.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then the autopilot state will eventually become NOMINAL. This is captured by the LTL template G(bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))), where bool_exp2 represents the condition 'supported & good' and bool_exp3 represents the condition 'state_is_NOMINAL'.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and the sensor data is good (bool_exp2), and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp1), then the autopilot will eventually change to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3) or the NOMINAL state will be entered.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change its state from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. This is captured by the template G (bool_exp2 -> (X ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3)))), where bool_exp2 represents the condition 'supported & good', and bool_exp3 represents 'state_is_NOMINAL'. The 'X' operator ensures that 'state_is_NOMINAL' is true at the next state after bool_exp2 becomes true. The eventual change of state is ensured by the future operator within the G template.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the state will transition to NOMINAL (bool_exp3) either immediately or in the future, within a bounded time frame, or the state will always be NOMINAL.

This LTL property states that globally, if the state is TRANSITION, the state will eventually become NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good. The property uses the `G` (Globally) and `F` (Eventually) operators to express this.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, and the current state is TRANSITION, then eventually the state will be NOMINAL. The G operator ensures this holds true at every point in time.  The bool_exp1 and bool_exp3 represent the conditions described in the natural language. N_DURATION is not relevant to the property.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the autopilot will eventually (F) transition to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3). N_DURATION is set to 1 to denote the next state transition

This LTL property states that if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL, or will remain in NOMINAL. The formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true at all times. bool_exp1 is defined as supported & good, bool_exp2 as state_is_TRANSITION, and bool_exp3 as state_is_NOMINAL, so that G((supported & good) -> (state_is_TRANSITION -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] state_is_NOMINAL) | (G state_is_NOMINAL)))). N_DURATION must be a positive integer, and is set to 1 here.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually, the autopilot state will transition to NOMINAL. The use of 'V' (release) ensures that NOMINAL is reached, or the condition that triggers the change remains true indefinitely. The template is chosen to capture the timing requirement.

The LTL property ensures that if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state.  The property uses a combination of implication and the eventually operator to capture this requirement.

This LTL property states that globally, if the autopilot state is TRANSITION and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot state will eventually be NOMINAL.  The use of 'G' ensures that if supported and good are true while TRANSITION is true then NOMINAL will eventually become true.

This LTL property states that globally, if the current state is TRANSITION, and both the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then the next state will be NOMINAL. This ensures the autopilot transitions to the NOMINAL state when the specified conditions are met.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is supported and the sensor data is good (bool_exp2), then the autopilot state will eventually transition from TRANSITION to NOMINAL (bool_exp3). The property uses the 'G' (Globally) and 'F' (Future) operators to express this temporal behavior. Specifically, the LTL ensures that, whenever the conditions are met, a transition from the TRANSITION to NOMINAL state will occur.
